writer.name: 费德里科·莫提卡 Federico Mottica

introduction: An educational documentary involving music and exclusive interviews with Holocaust survivors, the project shares memories of musical activities in the Theresienstadt ghetto-camp, a unique camp located in Czechoslovakia, where the presence of talented artists, musicians, and composers encouraged cultural life to flourish despite the desperate living conditions of the inmates. The documentary follows Sofia Tapinassi's intense journey studying the music of composers who were imprisoned in Theresienstadt such as Pavel Haas, Gideon Klein, and the more recently re-discovered Hans Winterberg, while she is also committed to meet some of the last survivors. The interviews reveal an inspirational story of inter-generational meeting that keep the memory of the Holocaust alive.
